reference: Hello, having periods twice a month means a shorter menstrual cycle of less than twenty days duration. The normal range for a menstrual cycle is twenty-one to thirty-five days. You are experiencing polymenorrhea, which, depending upon your symptoms and other health issues, should be addressed; otherwise, you may become anemic and weak. Please meet with your gynecologist and get a blood test for hemoglobin along with a transvaginal ultrasound and other investigations as required to rule out the causes of polymenorrhea. Obtain proper treatment for the underlying cause to regulate and normalize your menstrual cycle. It is possible to get pregnant with irregular periods by maintaining a menstrual calendar and having sexual intercourse regularly from four days before to four days after the day of ovulation. The day of ovulation can be calculated by subtracting fourteen from the date of the start of the next menstrual cycle, if you can predict it by maintaining a menstrual calendar. Having regular intercourse for sixteen months without success is not normal, and the reasons for being unsuccessful at conceiving should be investigated and treated. Usually, pregnancy occurs within twelve months of regular unprotected intercourse, and both partners should cooperate. The male partner should undergo a seminal fluid analysis.
text: Hello, having periods twice a month means a shorter menstrual cycle of less than 20 days duration. The normal range for a menstrual cycle is 21 to 5 days. You are experiencing polymenorrhea which, depending upon your symptoms and other health issues, should be addressed. Otherwise, you may become anemic and weak. Please meet with a gynecologist and get a blood test for hemoglobin along with a transvaginal ultrasound and other investigations as required to rule out the causes of polymenorrhea. Obtain proper treatment for the underlying cause to regulate and normalize your menstrual cycle. It is possible to get pregnant with irregular periods by maintaining a menstrual calendar and having sexual intercourse regularly from four days before to four days after the day of ovulation. The day of ovulation can be calculated by subtracting 14 from the date of the start of the next menstrual cycle, if you can predict it by maintaining a menstrual calendar. Having regular intercourse for 16 months without success is not normal and the reasons for being unsuccessful at conceiving should be investigated and usually pregnancy occurs within 12 months of regular unprotected intercourse and both partners should cooperate. The male partner should undergo a seminal fluid analysis.
